treatment_information.treatment_response: Markedly decreased continuous spikes and waves (CSW), ESES was controlled in 4 months, but spikes remained in the left occipital region.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Describing out loud how the strokes are made while writing each Kanji, and construction of Kanjis using the radicals.
treatment_information.treatment_response: Kanji dysgraphia ameliorated gradually several years after CSW was controlled, but the patient remained weak in his ability to write Kanji at high school.

treatment_timeline.event: Onset of generalized tonic-clonic seizures during sleep and increased errors in Kanji writing tests.
Admission for detailed work-up, overnight EEG showed generalized 1-2-Hz CSW dominant in the occipito-temporal region.
Treatment with sodium valproate, clonazepam, and acetazolamide started.
ESES controlled in 4 months, but spikes remained in the left occipital region.
EEG was normalized, and clinical seizure and epileptic discharge did not recur even without antiepileptic drugs.

patient_outcome.final_status: EEG was normalized, and clinical seizure and epileptic discharge did not recur even without antiepileptic drugs. Kanji dysgraphia ameliorated gradually several years after CSW was controlled, but the patient remained weak in his ability to write Kanji at high school.
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Several years after CSW was controlled for Kanji dysgraphia to ameliorate.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Remained weak in his ability to write Kanji at high school.
